input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. This is a known breaking change — `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` in API 30+. In .NET 10 (MAUI), the recommended approach changed.

Let me provide a concise, helpful answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI evolution.

In .NET 10 / MAUI, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach was replaced. The recommended solution is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior or handle it via the `Edge-to-Edge` / `WindowInsets` approach on Android.


The core issue is that `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` got deprecated at the Android platform level, so MAUI had to pivot away from it. The modern approach relies on `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` which is typically enabled by default in newer versions, or you can manually manage insets with edge-to-edge support if you need more control.
